nodes.label: AI as literature discovery tool
nodes.grounding_span: I looked to use AI to find papers to answer questions I had, however the results of this were very poor

nodes.label: AI for summarization and research
nodes.grounding_span: I did however start using AI to summarise papers instead of directly finding them to speed up the decision

nodes.label: AI as code generation assistant
nodes.grounding_span: AI was helpful in writing the code

nodes.label: Thorough verification of AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I often had smaller sub sets of data that were of a size a human could parse them. I then ran test cases to ensure the output was correct

nodes.label: Research efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I could skim through a greater number this way, wasting less time

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I knew it existed and was accurate from having read it

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI for literature search
nodes.grounding_span: the results of this were very poor and often more of a hinderance than a help

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI summaries
nodes.grounding_span: I did however start using AI to summarise papers

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for code generation
nodes.grounding_span: AI was helpful in writing the code

nodes.label: Conditional trust based on verifiability
nodes.grounding_span: I often didn't. If the AI said there was nothing of use I moved on quickly. But if it said there was useful items I went and read it myself

nodes.label: Methodical verification through testing and line-by-line review
nodes.grounding_span: I then ran test cases to ensure the output was correct. I also manually implemented print and check statements

nodes.label: Probing questioning to debug AI reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: I asked for it to summarise findings related to a small area
